mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong demand for residential construction, boosting corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite HOUST growth.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, increasing corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint movement of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to predict SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05) in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding supply-side capacity, reducing inflationary pressures and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of triggered INDPRO growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: High federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) during a tightening cycle signal reduced liquidity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of high FEDFUNDS periods despite tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% indicates a tight labor market, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, which negatively impacts S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE < 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month surge in real sales (RSAFS) reflects strong consumer demand, increasing corporate revenues and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 60% of triggered RSAFS growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in CPI (CPIAUCSL) below 2% signals disinflation, prompting central banks to maintain accommodative policies and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL < 2%.
